description.overview: Australian Cattle Dogs are tough, compact herding dogs bred to drive cattle across rough terrain. They're incredibly intelligent and hardworking.
description.temperament: ACDs are loyal, protective, and wary of strangers. They're devoted to their owners and need jobs to do. May nip at heels (herding instinct).
description.health: Generally very healthy and long-lived. Watch for hip dysplasia, deafness, and eye problems.
description.exercise: Very high exercise needs. Need vigorous daily activity and mental challenges. Excel at herding and dog sports.

verdict.summary: Tough, intelligent working dogs that need extensive exercise and mental challenges. ACDs are loyal and protective but need experienced owners. Best for active rural/suburban living with jobs to do.
